title: Domestic Violence: Legal Protection and Remedies in Bangladesh
metaTitle: Domestic Violence Legal Protection Bangladesh – Rights & Remedies 2025
metaDescription: Experiencing domestic violence in Bangladesh? Know your legal rights under the Domestic Violence Act 2010 — protection orders, shelter, maintenance, and how to take legal action.
heroIntro: Domestic violence is a recognised legal harm in Bangladesh under the Domestic Violence (Prevention and Protection) Act 2010. Victims — primarily women and children — have access to immediate legal protection through court orders, shelter homes, and criminal prosecution of abusers.
toc: What is Domestic Violence Under Bangladesh Law?
How to Apply for a Protection Order

sections.h2: What is Domestic Violence Under Bangladesh Law?
sections.content:
The Domestic Violence (Prevention and Protection) Act 2010 defines domestic violence broadly to include:

  • Physical abuse: Any act of bodily harm, assault, or use of force
  • Sexual abuse: Forced sexual acts within marriage or the household
  • Emotional/psychological abuse: Threats, humiliation, isolation, controlling behaviour
  • Economic abuse: Withholding money, preventing employment, controlling assets

Both ongoing relationships and post-separation situations are covered. A family lawyer in Uttara can assess your situation and recommend the fastest legal route.

The Act protects:

  • Wife or former wife
  • Female partner in a live-in relationship
  • Female family members of the household
  • Children of the household

Male victims of domestic violence can still seek remedies under the Penal Code (assault, grievous hurt) even though the DV Act 2010 primarily protects women and children.

A court can issue:

  • Protection Order: Prevents the abuser from committing further violence, contacting, or approaching the victim
  • Residence Order: Prevents the abuser from entering the shared home or allows the victim to remain in the home
  • Monetary Relief Order: Requires the abuser to pay for medical expenses, loss of earnings, or maintenance
  • Custody Order: Temporary custody of children to the victim pending full hearing
  • Compensation Order: Payment for pain, suffering, and emotional distress

sections.h2: How to Apply for a Protection Order
sections.content:
1. File an application before the Chief Metropolitan Magistrate (in cities) or the Chief Judicial Magistrate (in districts) — or the Family Court.
  2. Alternatively, report to a Protection Officer (government-appointed officer under the DV Act) who will assist in filing.
  3. The court can issue an emergency or ex-parte order on the same day if there is imminent danger — without hearing the other side first.
  4. Final hearing with both parties — court makes the permanent order.

Evidence to gather: medical reports, photos of injuries, witness statements, WhatsApp/call records showing threats.

Civil protection orders and criminal prosecution can run simultaneously. File an FIR at the police station for:

  • Section 323/325 Penal Code: causing hurt or grievous hurt
  • Section 498A Penal Code: cruelty by husband or relatives
  • Women and Children Repression Prevention Act 2000: physical or sexual violence against women

Criminal prosecution leads to imprisonment of the abuser, while civil orders provide immediate protection and financial relief. Use both pathways for maximum protection. Contact Advocate Md. Shah Alam for urgent legal help.

faqs.question: Can I get a protection order without a lawyer in Bangladesh?
faqs.answer: Yes. You can directly approach the Protection Officer or the Magistrate Court. However, a lawyer significantly improves your chances of getting an emergency same-day order and ensures all evidence is properly presented.

faqs.question: What emergency helplines are available for domestic violence victims in Bangladesh?
faqs.answer: National Emergency: 999 | Women and Children Helpline: 109 | Bangladesh National Women Lawyers' Association (BNWLA): 01711-037893 | One-Stop Crisis Centres (OCC) at district hospitals provide free medical, legal, and shelter assistance.

faqs.question: Can I file a domestic violence case if I have already left the shared home?
faqs.answer: Yes. You can file for protection orders, maintenance, and criminal prosecution even after leaving the shared household. The Act still applies to past incidents and ongoing threats.

faqs.question: Will a protection order prevent my husband from seeing our children?
faqs.answer: Not automatically. A temporary custody order can be included in the protection order application. The court will balance the children's welfare with both parents' access rights.
